abstract: Bicycloalkanes, cubanes, and their structural derivatives have gained significant prominence as sp3-rich bioisosteres of (hetero)arenes, offering a robust strategy to improve the metabolic stability and solubility of drug candidates. To satisfy the growing need for these complex 3D scaffolds, the chemical community has pioneered a wide range of innovative synthetic strategies. This review surveys the recent advancements in our ability to construct and functionalize these high-value molecules using light-driven processes. We examine diverse structural targets and focus on reaction manifolds that provide access to: (i) [1.1.1]-bicyclopentanes (BCPs); (ii) [2.2.1]-bicyclohexanes (BCHs); (iii) [3.1.1]-bicycloheptanes (BCHeps); and (iv) cubanes, alongside other structurally related motifs. Finally, we discuss emerging trends and future perspectives on identifying new reaction paradigms to unlock further functionalized bioisosteric units for medicinal and materials science.
